table_semantics.justification: Full table structure with <caption>, <thead>, <tbody>, <th scope='col'> for plan names, and <th scope='row'> for every feature row. No layout misuse.

element_choice.justification:
CTAs correctly use elements (navigate to /signup and /contact-sales), prices in

, plan cards use

, lists use
    /
  • . No div/span substituting for semantic elements.

heading_hierarchy.justification: Single h1 'Pricing Plans', logical h2 sections ('Available Plans', 'Feature Comparison'), h3 for plan names within the h2 section. No skipped levels; prices are not headings.

aria_discipline.justification: ARIA used only where needed: aria-labelledby on sections, aria-label on nav and disambiguating link labels, aria-hidden on decorative duplicate text (visible 'Most Popular' p, visible Yes/No spans), visually-hidden spans provide AT-friendly alternatives. No redundant role attributes.

landmark_structure.justification: Skip link present targeting <main id='main-content'>, <header>, labelled <nav aria-label='Primary'>, <main>, <footer> all used correctly. Sections carry aria-labelledby. tabindex='-1' on main for skip link target.

list_semantics.justification: Plan cards correctly wrapped in <ul>/<li> so count is communicated to AT. No lists used for non-list content.

content_realism.justification: Pricing, plan names, and feature values are realistic. Intro paragraph is natural. Company name is a generic placeholder ('Your Company').

expectations_met: Feature comparison uses <table> for two-dimensional data
Plan names are <th scope='col'> in the header row
Feature names are <th scope='row'> in each data row
Yes/No values conveyed via text for both visual and AT users (visible + visually-hidden pattern)
'Most Popular' badge is accessible via visually-hidden span inside the h3
CTA elements use <a> since they navigate to pages
'Contact Sales' is a link pointing to /contact-sales
Plan names inside card articles use h3 (correct under h2 parent section)
Prices are not headings — wrapped in
Boolean features use plain text, no icon-only representations
additional_observations: The pattern of aria-hidden on visible 'Yes'/'No' text with visually-hidden 'Included'/'Not included' alternatives is slightly unconventional but semantically correct and more descriptive for AT users.
tabindex='-1' on <main> is a good practice to ensure the skip link focus target works across all browsers.
